{"key_event":"Postwar developmental colonialism and urban associational growth","reasoning":"The Ten-Year Plan, export boom, infrastructure expansion, and deeper administrative reach pushed an already powerful Belgian colonial state into the strong range, despite some retreat from overt wartime labor coercion. Civil society rose from near-atomization as urban workers, churches, mutual-aid groups, ABAKO, Kimbanguist networks, schooling, and an évolué layer expanded, but surveillance, co-optation, and repression kept it only barely above the atomized range.","society_change":0.9,"society_power":2.1,"state_change":0.5,"state_power":8.4}
